statement.markdown: Write a function that validates a Git tag name. A valid Git tag name consists of uppercase and lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. The tag name must not start or end with a hyphen. The function should return `true` if the tag name is valid and `false` otherwise.
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n) where n is the length of the tag name. Space complexity: O(n) for the input string.

examples.explanation: Invalid Git tag name (ends with a hyphen)
Invalid Git tag name (contains a hyphen at the start)

hints.text: Consider using a regular expression to match the valid tag name pattern.

editorial.title: Correcting Git Tag Name Validation
editorial.intuition: To validate a Git tag name, we need to check if it consists of uppercase and lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens, and does not start or end with a hyphen.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use a brute force approach to check each character of the tag name and see if it matches the valid pattern.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use a regular expression to match the valid tag name pattern.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to match the valid tag name pattern.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by checking all possible cases of valid and invalid tag names.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: input = 'v1.2', output = true
commonMistakes: Mistaking a hyphen at the start or end as a valid character

starterCode.javascript: // Write a function that validates a Git tag name
